content:
Hello everyone , I am working on a complex struct in Rust , to be exactly precise it looks like something like this in C :

struct ce_gw_job {
/* -- Snip --*/
union {
struct net_device *dev;
} src; /**< CAN / ETH frame data source */
union {
struct net_device *dev;
} dst; /**< CAN / ETH frame data destination */
union {
struct can_filter can_rcv_filter;
/* TODO: Add ethernet receive filter (eth_rcv_filter) */
};
and I couldn't figure out how to impement the struct in Rust . Any insights ? Ps : I can't use Option and Box here because I need this struct to be implemented in another macro that requires the struct to be as it is !
Those look like unions with only one field? I don't think that has any effect ...

I am not really sure , I am just trying to translate the struct into rust for my project but what I understand from C unions is that they are used to make all the elements share the same memory . Is it possible to ignore it in this case ?
Yes, but C unions only do that when you have several fields in a single union. When they are separate unions, they don't share the memory.
At least that's how I understand it.
That makes a lot of sense , thank you for your help!!
They may want to support having multiple source/destination/filter types down the line, other than just net_device. Which we would probably represent as traits like FrameSource/FrameDst
But no need to worry about that now
